text.title: Kubernetes Ingress Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the lb-kubernetes-ingress methodology end-to-end.

step.name: pick-controller
step.input: Cluster type, existing knowledge, traffic profile.
step.action: Apply 06-decision-tree.xml. ingress-nginx for general HTTP/HTTPS; haproxy-ingress for TCP+rate-limit + stick-tables; traefik for cert-manager-free + dashboard; envoy-gateway for Gateway API + traffic splitting.

step.name: emit-controller-deployment
step.input: Controller choice + node count + AZ count.
step.action: Render `templates/controller-deployment.yaml` with `replicas: 3`, PDB `minAvailable: 2`, `podAntiAffinity` on hostname, `topologySpreadConstraints` on zone, `terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 300`, explicit `resources.requests` + `limits`.

step.name: emit-per-app-ingress
step.input: App routes + TLS issuer + per-route policy (rate-limit / CORS / body size).
step.action: For each app, render `templates/ingress.yaml` with `ingressClassName` matching the controller, `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer` annotation for TLS, plus rate-limit + CORS + body-size annotations from the controller's own namespace.
step.decision-gate: If any annotation uses a different controller's namespace, fail.

step.name: separate-classes
step.action: For each class (public / internal / admin), deploy a separate controller Deployment + IngressClass. Public class binds to the public LoadBalancer; internal class binds to the internal LB or ClusterIP only.

step.name: validate-and-drain-test
step.action: Run `validate-lb-kubernetes-ingress.py --file artefact.json`. Drain a controller-node: `kubectl drain node-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data`. Watch that traffic stays green (no 502s) — PDB + anti-affinity should keep ≥ 2 controllers up at all times.
